label: IEEE Transactions on Medical Imaging
description: 近 45 天 TMI 期刊 MRI 相关论文（2026-05-22 ~ 2026-07-06，共 7 篇，已下载开放获取 PDF 0 篇）

items.title: Biomechanics-Informed Non-Rigid Medical Image Registration With Elasticity Theories
items.description: Biomechanical modelling of soft tissue provides a method for constraining medical image registration, such that the estimated spatial transformation is considered biophysically plausible. Existing methods either directly optimize the loss function containing the biomechanical-constrained regularization term over deform…
items.authors: Zhe Min, Zachary M. C. Baum, Shaheer U. Saeed, Shixing Ma, et al.

items.title: Temporal Conditioning for Longitudinal Brain MRI Registration and Aging Analysis
items.description: Longitudinal brain analysis is essential for understanding healthy aging and identifying pathological deviations. Longitudinal registration of sequential brain MRI underpins such analyses. However, existing methods are limited by reliance on densely sampled time series, a trade-off between accuracy and temporal smoothn…
items.authors: Bailiang Jian, Jiazhen Pan, Yitong Li, Fabian Bongratz, et al.

items.title: Cogformer: A Unified Multi-Scale Brain Representation for Visual Decoding and Reconstruction From fMRI
items.description: With the rapid development of deep generative models (DGMs), the performance of decoding language and reconstructing images from Functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI) has been improved. Nevertheless, the accurate representation of brain activity remains highly challenging, primarily due to the limited paired sam…
items.authors: Xu Yin, John Q. Gan, Haixian Wang

items.title: From Contrast-Driven Segmentation to Central Lumbar Spinal Stenosis Grading: A Comprehensive Multi-View Spinal MRI Image Analysis
items.description: Central lumbar spinal stenosis, a prevalent degenerative spinal disorder, severely impacts the quality of life for those affected. Axial and sagittal MRI images offer diverse information on tissue structure and lesions, which is crucial for accurate diagnosis. However, MRI-based diagnostic approaches still have poor le…
items.authors: Zhengchao Zhou, Pingping Wang, Xinggui Ji, Wanbo Xu, et al.

items.title: Few-Shot Contrastive Learning for Cross-Task Stroke Prognosis Prediction With Multimodal Data
items.description: Predicting stroke outcome remains challenging due to inherent heterogeneity, misalignment of multimodal clinical data, and the availability of well-annotated longitudinal datasets. Current methodologies often lack robustness and generalizability across these tasks. We propose a few-shot contrastive learning framework t…
items.authors: Haoran Peng, Yuxiang Dai, Rencheng Zheng, Mingming Wang, et al.

items.title: Axon Diameter Mapping From Myelin Water Diffusion MRI
items.description: Probing diffusion in myelin water using diffusion-weighted ${T} {_{{1}}}$ -/ ${T} {_{{2}}}$ -selective MRI acquisitions enables noninvasive measurement of myelinated axon diameter. Its application for in vivo measurements requires numerical verification through diffusion simulations. Here, we propose the theory of myel…
items.authors: Hong-Hsi Lee, Kwok-Shing Chan, Dmitry S. Novikov, Els Fieremans, et al.

items.title: MambaMatch: A Novel Model for Semi-Supervised Spinal Cortical and Cancellous Bone Segmentation
items.description: Precise cortical and cancellous bone segmentation is essential for safe laminectomy. However, it remains challenging due to limited annotations and high anatomical similarity. To mitigate these challenges, we propose MambaMatch, an end-to-end semi-supervised segmentation framework collaboratively optimized under a teac…
items.authors: Lianliang Li, Xue Li, Wenxin Chen, Sida Lyu, et al.
